svg.text: Build-agnostic model of every role, its atomic steps, and the connections between them.
The only role with direct user contact. Kickoff, escalation handling at the highest tier, final
Receive user prompt; create the run substrate at runs/{slug}/.
Dispatch Discovery (Initial Mode) with the prompt.
On Discovery returning a ledger: dispatch TD (Initial Mode).
On TD returning sections + contracts: dispatch Editor.
On Editor pass: dispatch Coordinator with the dispatch plan.
Monitor for escalations; route Sev-4 to Demotion Mode, never to user.
On Coordinator build-complete: dispatch Integrator, then Critic final sweep, then CV.
On CV verdict: write run-report.md, finalize delivery, mark phase tasks complete.
• Only role allowed to surface to the user (at delivery, not mid-build).
• Does not make content decisions — delegates to Discovery / TD / etc.
patterns within a section — that is
Re-engaged on escalation. Produces a delta
plan amending the build without forcing a full
• Does not enact the delta plan — that is
Audits TD's output against the user's literal prompt and Discovery's atomic intent. Structural, not
Read the user's literal prompt and Discovery's ledger.
Check: does the plan address every proper noun and every first-contact requirement?
Check: does the plan honor the telos? Any inflection-point default that contradicts the telos?
Check: are there structural gaps between Discovery and TD that would mean the build ships
Return verdict: pass / pass_with_recommendations / fail — write
• Structural check, not substantive — does not propose new IPs or assumptions.
Flow control. Builds the dependency DAG, dispatches sections in waves, monitors progress, enacts
delta plans on re-evaluation. Makes no architectural or product decisions.
Pick dispatch_mode (inline / nested / sub-agent) per ≤8 / >8 / explicit threshold.
Dispatch Wave 1; verify each section against contract before next wave.
On TD impact delta plan: enact (no-op for unaffected / re-dispatch for salvageable / new
Overseer for new sections / amend contracts).
On all sections verified + Integrator complete: write state/coordinator/build-complete.json.
• Makes no architectural or product decisions.
• Cannot amend ledger / sections / contracts substantively.
Decompose into 1-N Builder tasks per the
Verify each Builder output against the
On all builders complete: mark section
On any block: write escalation packet to
• Does not amend Discovery's ledger or TD's
Narrow scope, one output file (or small
handful) per dispatch. Completes one specific
Read your task briefing + your section
Implement exactly the scope in the
briefing; do not expand to adjacent
ignored — out-of-scope is the most common
Assembles section outputs into a single artifact. Single-pass, no agent collaboration.
Read every section output from output/builders/* and every contract from contracts/*.
Resolve import paths and inter-file references.
Assemble the final artifact at output/integration/.
Apply Sev-0 fixes inline (whitespace, trivial naming) without escalating.
Write output/integration/manifest.json describing the assembled file tree.
On unresolvable issue: write escalation packet.
• Sev-0 fixes only inline; anything substantive escalates.
Detects drift and inconsistency. Scheduled audits during build +
Detect drift between charter, ledger, sections plan,
Severity-classify each finding (Sev 0-3 in scheduled, Sev
Final-sweep mode: 9 structural checks before CV — coverage
holds, no unannotated contract drift, no inline-deviation
For Sev ≥ medium: write audit/flags.jsonl + emit
• Detects drift; does not decide what is correct. Routes via
Exercises the artifact under production fidelity. Multi-tier verification: first-contact,
prompt-named-verb, then assumptions and edge cases.
Read ledger + sections plan + contracts + Integrator's manifest.
Configure production-fidelity environment (real components for everything the artifact loads at
Run Tier 2 first-contact verifications (artifact loads, primary surface visible).
On Tier 2 pass: verify the prompt-named verb against canonical evidence.
Run assumption checks and out-of-scope checks.
Write output/verification/report.json with verdict + per-check evidence.
• Production fidelity is mandatory; modeled components require external source citations.
• Verifies; does not redesign. Wrong assertions escalate, not silently amend.
Event-driven. Classifies escalations and routes. Makes no
Wake on new files in state/escalations/queue/ OR Critic
Classify the escalation: structural / decision / runtime /
Determine severity (Sev 0-4) and target role for
• Makes no content decisions; classification + routing only.
Dispatched by TD to investigate inflection points. Returns
Investigate the IP candidates against canonical
Compare pros and cons; recommend one with rationale.
• Researches but does not decide — recommends with
• Cannot modify state files outside research/probes/.
Dispatched by Arbiter to investigate problems that escaped a
Read Arbiter's escalation packet and the blast-radius
Optimize for blast-radius minimization: prefer surgical
fixes preserving in-flight work over wholesale
Write findings; Arbiter routes from there.
• Cannot enact the fix — that is Coordinator (after
Background role. Maintains history/log.jsonl as canonical causal
record with rationale captured. Excluded from Completion
Subscribe to every state-changing file event across the
For each event: capture the actor, target file, summary,
Append to history/log.jsonl as a single JSON line.
IDs to their authoritative source files.
Audits a prior run under a newer architecture version.
Recommends recovery action; does not enact.
Read the target run + the architecture version to audit
For each principle and structural rule that differs from
when the run was built: check conformance.
For non-conformances: classify as patchable, salvageable,
• Recommends only. Orchestrator decides whether to enact
• One reaudit file per architecture version.
